evals.expected_output: Should identify stored context in struct, replacing request context with Background, leaked cancel func, string-matched error handling, loss of sentinel wrapping/inspection, log-and-swallow returning nil,nil, and unnecessary else after return.
expectations: Finds the stored context in UserService and/or replacing request context with context.Background as a real issue.
Finds string-matched error handling and destroyed error-inspection contract.
Finds the log-and-swallow path that returns nil, nil on error.

evals.expected_output: Should identify value receivers on a type containing mutex/map causing copylock semantics and misleading method set, mutable slice aliasing on Get/Set, avoidable pointer-to-mutex and pointer-to-config zero-value friendliness issues, and assess IsReady naming/zero-value contract clarity.
expectations: Finds value receiver copy-safety problems on a type containing mutex-protected state.
Finds mutable []byte ownership or aliasing leak across Get and/or Set.
Flags zero-value or pointer-heavy surface issues on the exported Client shape.

evals.expected_output: Should identify mutable exported global plus init side effects, awkward *http.Header API instead of http.Header value, possible nil map write on *h, direct map assignment bypassing Header helpers/canonicalization/add semantics, and optionally note zero-value/nil map behavior in CloneMap contract if relevant.
expectations: Finds mutable exported global state and/or init side effects as a package-surface risk.
Finds the awkward pointer-to-http.Header API shape or nil-map-write risk.
Finds raw header map mutation bypassing http.Header helper semantics.

evals.expected_output: Should identify returning a typed-nil pointer through an interface as a real exported API bug, explain why callers see a non-nil interface, and optionally flag nil-job panic risk on Run.
expectations: Finds the typed-nil interface return as a real exported API bug.
Explains why callers can observe a non-nil interface even though the concrete pointer is nil.
Keeps the review centered on caller-visible API and runtime behavior rather than style.

evals.expected_output: Should identify map iteration order assumption as a correctness issue for a stable key helper, explain caller-visible instability, and give a deterministic fix direction. Stronger answers may also notice delimiter-collision risk on the exported string contract.
expectations: Finds map iteration order assumption as a correctness problem for a stable exported helper.
Explains caller-visible instability or cache-key mismatch risk from non-deterministic map order.
Provides a concrete deterministic fix direction.

evals.expected_output: Should identify missing scanner.Err check as a correctness bug, explain why loop completion does not prove success, and ideally mention bufio.Scanner token-limit behavior as residual risk.
expectations: Finds missing scanner.Err check as a correctness issue.
Explains why loop completion without checking scanner.Err is not proof of success.
Keeps the review in the idiomatic Go lane rather than broad architecture commentary.
